scope.summary: Plan §30.4 item 8's second half, plus a real gap it exposed: the pinned canonical-tag list named 7 AuditOutcome variants while the enum has 8, so HeldUncommitted (Arc I, tag 7) was never pinned.
out_of_scope: The preimage KAT half of item 8, already covered by the v5/v7/v8/v9 canonical-preimage assertions in the same file.

The DI1 held-execute regression itself (§30.4 item 2, exactly-one-audit-entry under a late deadline). This close pins the audit ENCODING of the HeldUncommitted outcome, not the dispatcher behaviour that produces it.

A literal compile-time assertion. Rust has no variant reflection, so the compile-time half is an in-crate exhaustive match that fails to build when a variant is added, and the prefix comparison itself runs as a unit test.

entry_points.name: cargo test -p oraclemcp-audit
entry_points.trace: 239 lib tests pass, including the three tag tests. v5_enum_tags_are_stable_and_unique now pins all 8 outcome tags (it listed 7; HeldUncommitted=7 was absent). canonical_tags_are_injective_across_every_variant walks the exhaustive const variant lists and asserts no two variants share a canonical tag. no_variant_name_prefixes_another asserts no Debug or serde name is a prefix of another, for both enums.

entry_points.name: in-crate exhaustive match (all_decisions / all_outcomes)
entry_points.trace: Both const fns carry an exhaustive match over their enum. #[non_exhaustive] does not apply inside the defining crate, so adding a variant stops the crate compiling until the list is extended — which is the property the hand-written list lacked when HeldUncommitted was added.

limitations: MUTATION-CHECKED for injectivity only: remapping HeldUncommitted onto RolledBack's tag 3 fails canonical_tags_are_injective_across_every_variant with '[0,1,2,3,4,5,6,3]'. The prefix assertion was not mutation-checked by renaming a public variant, because that cascades through every match in the workspace; its logic is a direct starts_with comparison over the same exhaustive lists.

The v5+ preimage hashes these enums as numeric tags and length-frames variable-length fields, so the prefix property is defence-in-depth for unframed surfaces (serde output, logs, refusal corpus, CEF export), not a live preimage hazard today.
Scoped verification: cargo test -p oraclemcp-audit and cargo clippy -p oraclemcp-audit --all-targets. No workspace-wide run.
